author: JB
text:
# Slice S1: Authz hardening + role-management

You are implementing this slice in isolation in your own git worktree. Build all described behavior, write tests, and commit when done. Never block or wait for input — decide and continue.

## Backlog items

### F-08 — Enforce role-based authorization on all mutating endpoints

**Rationale:** All mutations are gated by authenticate only; requireRole exists in middleware but is unused, so a viewer JWT can create/update/delete items, post checkouts and returns, and read the full user directory. Merges the umbrella authz item with the item-write and checkout-write sub-items.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A viewer-role JWT calling POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/items receives 403 and the item set is unchanged
- A viewer-role JWT calling POST /api/checkouts or POST /api/checkouts/:id/return receives 403 and item quantity is unchanged
- A member-role JWT calling POST /api/items receives 201; admin/member writes still succeed
- An admin-role JWT calling GET /api/users receives 200 while a viewer receives 403; all GET item reads still succeed for a viewer (200)

**Hint files (touch-set):** `src/server/routes/items.ts`, `src/server/routes/checkouts.ts`, `src/server/routes/users.ts`, `src/server/auth/middleware.ts`

### F-10 — Admin-only role-management endpoint

**Rationale:** There is no way to change a user's role; the Users page is read-only and no mutation route exists. Role changes must be admin-gated and must not let a caller escalate themselves via the body.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- An admin can change another user's role to a value in {admin,member,viewer} and a subsequent read reflects it
- A member or viewer attempting the role change returns 403 and no role changes
- A request body supplying an invalid role string is rejected (400) with no change
- A non-admin cannot escalate their own role by calling the endpoint

**Hint files (touch-set):** `src/server/routes/users.ts`, `src/client/api.ts`, `src/shared/types.ts`, `src/client/containers/UsersPage.tsx`

### F-09 — Restrict the Users directory to admins on the client (route guard + nav)

**Rationale:** The client /users route and nav link are visible to everyone even once the server is hardened; listing should be admin-only on the client too. Server-side gating lives in F-08.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A logged-in viewer or member who navigates to /users does not see the user table (redirected or shown a forbidden state)
- The Users nav link is not rendered for non-admin users
- An admin sees the Users nav link and the populated table
- GET /api/users with a member or viewer token returns 403 while an admin gets 200 with the seeded 4 users

**Hint files (touch-set):** `src/server/routes/users.ts`, `src/client/routes.tsx`, `src/client/containers/Layout.tsx`, `src/client/containers/UsersPage.tsx`

---
Work only within your assigned slice. Do not modify files outside your slice's scope.

author: JB
text: **Notes:** - `middleware.ts` (a hint file) needed no change — `requireRole` already existed and was correct; the gap was simply that it was unused. - Touched `src/client/hooks/useUsers.ts` (not in the explicit hint list) because CLAUDE.md mandates client data access go through the TanStack Query hooks — that's the right home for the role mutation. - All HTTP responses still funnel through `serialize.ts` (`ok`/`fail`/`serializeUser`), per the single-chokepoint convention.

Work only within your assigned slice. Do not modify files outside your slice's scope.

author: JB
text: Two notes on scope: I left `src/shared/types.ts` untouched since `Role`/`ROLES` already provided everything F-10 needed, and I added the role mutation to the existing `useUsers.ts` hook (per the "data access via hooks" convention) rather than inlining the network call in the page.

Work only within your assigned slice. Do not modify files outside your slice's scope.

author: JB
text: One note on scope: F-09's client behavior (route guard, nav visibility, role-edit UI) isn't unit-tested — the project's vitest setup is node-only with no React Testing Library/jsdom installed, so I verified it via typecheck + production build rather than adding a new test toolchain outside the slice. The server-side gate that backs F-09 *is* covered by tests.

author: JB
text: One note: F-09's acceptance criteria are client-rendering behaviors and the repo has no client/jsdom test harness (vitest is node-only, no testing-library), so those are covered by implementation + the server-side 403 checks rather than a component test. Its server portion (GET `/api/users` → 403 for member/viewer, 200 for admin with 4 users) is tested.
